item.title: End-to-end smoke test — run full user journey against real Docker on local machine
item.intent: 946 unit tests pass but nothing proves the software works. An e2e smoke test must run the actual user journey (install → init → build → up → doctor → down → destroy) against real Docker containers and real filesystem, catching every integration gap the unit tests hide.
item.description: Every external system interaction in ClawHQ is mocked in tests. Docker builds, container lifecycle, filesystem scaffolding, health checks, and config generation are tested in isolation but never together. The documented user journey has never been executed end-to-end. This is the LAUNCH GATE — a security tool with a security bug is dead on arrival. No compromises.
steps: Create test/e2e/ directory for end-to-end tests
Create a minimal test blueprint YAML that requires no external API keys (local Ollama or mock LLM)
Create a non-interactive init path or pre-canned config fixture that bypasses the wizard
Write smoke-test.sh or smoke-test.test.ts that executes each CLI command in sequence
Each step: capture exit code, stdout, stderr; assert expected outcomes (files created, container running, etc.)
Add npm run test:e2e script to package.json (separate from unit tests — requires Docker)
Run the smoke test, document every failure, add each as a backlog item
Integrate into CI as an optional job (requires Docker-in-Docker or privileged runner)
acceptanceCriteria: npm run test:e2e runs the full user journey: install → init → build → up → doctor → down → destroy
Test uses real Docker (not mocked) and real filesystem (temp directory, cleaned up after)
Test uses a minimal blueprint that requires no external API keys
Each step reports pass/fail with captured output for debugging
Test is separate from unit tests (does not run on npm test) because it requires Docker
Every failure found during initial run is filed as a backlog item
